session.user: Perform string literal replacements in Python test files. Do NOT use LibCST for this task - just read each file and replace specific string patterns using the Edit tool. ## Files to Update For each file below, replace the specified `old_string` with `new_string`. ### File 1: /home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/gateways/github/metadata_blocks/test_plan_wrapping.py Replace ALL occurrences of `erk-plan` with `erk-pr` (there are multiple, use replace_all=true). ### File 2: /home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/gateways/github/metadata_blocks/test_round_trip.py Replace: ``` "erk-plan": { "pr_number": 42, "worktree_name": "test-worktree", "timestamp": "2025-01-15T10:00:00Z", }, ``` with: ``` "erk-pr": { "pr_number": 42, "worktree_name": "test-worktree", "timestamp": "2025-01-15T10:00:00Z", }, ``` And replace: ``` "erk-plan": create_plan_block( pr_number=42, worktree_name="test-worktree", timestamp="2025-01-15T10:00:00Z", ), ``` with: ``` "erk-pr": create_plan_block( pr_number=42, worktree_name="test-worktree", timestamp="2025-01-15T10:00:00Z", ), ``` ### File 3: /home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/pr_store/test_planned_pr_backend.py Read the file first, then find any reference to `"erk-plan"` (as a label string, NOT `BlockKeys.ERK_PLAN`) and replace with `"erk-pr"`. Also replace `BlockKeys.ERK_PLAN` with `BlockKeys.ERK_PR`. ### File 4: /home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_remote_paths.py Read the file first. Replace: - `` with `` - `erk-plan` with `erk-pr` - `` with `` ### File 5: /home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_dispatch.py Read the file first. Replace any `erk-plan` label reference with `erk-pr`. Look for strings like `"erk-plan"` in the context of labels, NOT in comments/docstrings about the plan concept. ### File 6: /home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/dash/test_filtering.py Read the file first. Replace comment `# Must have erk-plan label to be returned by default` with `# Must have erk-pr label to be returned by default`. ### File 7: /home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/tui/test_plan_table.py Read the file first. Replace `labels=("erk-plan",)` with `labels=("erk-pr",)`. ### File 8: /home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/core/test_impl_issue_wt_workflow.py Read the file first. Find and update: - Any test function name/docstring about `erk_plan_label` or `erk-plan label` → update docstrings to say `erk-pr` - Any `"erk-plan"` label string assertions → replace with `"erk-pr"` ### File 9: /home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/integration/test_real_git_commit_ops.py Read the file first. Replace: - `# Assert: No erk-plan-*.idx files remain in our isolated temp dir` → `# Assert: No erk-pr-*.idx files remain in our isolated temp dir` - `remaining = list(test_temp_dir.glob("erk-plan-*.idx"))` → `remaining = list(test_temp_dir.glob("erk-pr-*.idx"))` ### File 10: /home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/tests/unit/github/test_objective_issues.py Read the file first. Replace comments/docstrings about `erk-plan` that say objectives don't get `erk-plan` label → say `erk-pr`: - `"""Objective issues use only erk-objective label (NOT erk-plan)."""` → `(NOT erk-pr)` - `# Verify labels only include erk-objective (NOT erk-plan)` → `(NOT erk-pr)` - `assert "erk-plan" not in fake_gh.labels` → `assert "erk-pr" not in fake_gh.labels` (there may be 2 occurrences) - `assert "erk-plan" not in labels` → `assert "erk-pr" not in labels` ### File 11: /home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-statusline/tests/test_statusline.py Read the file first. The statusline now accepts BOTH `erk_pr: true` and `erk_plan: true` (backward compat). The tests that use `erk_plan: true` in frontmatter should still pass since we kept backward compat. Only update the test descriptions/docstrings: - `"""Should find plan file with erk_plan: true."""` → `"""Should find plan file with erk_pr: true."""` - `"""Should return None when erk_plan is false."""` → `"""Should return None when erk_pr is false."""` Do NOT change the `erk_plan: true/false` content in the frontmatter strings - those test backward compatibility. Actually, on second thought for the statusline tests: Add a new test case using `erk_pr: true` format (by changing one of the existing cases to use `erk_pr: true` instead of `erk_plan: true`), so we have coverage for both formats. Specifically: - The first test case (line ~723) that has `erk_plan: true` in frontmatter → change to use `erk_pr: true` in the frontmatter AND update the docstring - Keep the remaining tests with `erk_plan: true` as-is (they test backward compat) Wait - actually don't add new tests. Just update docstrings and leave the frontmatter values as they are for backward compat testing. ### File 12: /home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/objective_issues/test_label_definitions.py Read the file first. There are two tests: 1. `test_get_erk_label_definitions_excludes_erk_plan` - asserts `"erk-plan" not in label_names` 2. `test_get_required_erk_labels_excludes_erk_plan` - asserts `"erk-plan" not in label_names` Per the plan: "test asserts `'erk-plan' not in labels`. Since `'erk-pr'` IS a valid label, this test assertion must change (verify label IS present or remove the negative assertion)" So for these tests: - Rename the test functions: `excludes_erk_plan` → `excludes_erk_pr_from_required_labels` or similar that makes sense - Update docstrings - Change `assert "erk-plan" not in label_names` to `assert "erk-pr" not in label_names` (since plans are identified by title prefix, not label) Wait, actually look at the context: the plan says `erk-pr` IS a valid label (it's the label applied to plan PRs). So "excludes erk-pr" from label definitions means the label definitions list doesn't include `erk-pr` as a label that needs to be created. Need to understand what `get_erk_label_definitions` returns vs what labels are used. Just check what the test is actually testing: if `get_erk_label_definitions()` is about labels that should be created in GitHub (like `erk-objective` etc), and if `erk-pr` is one of them or not. Read the test to understand before changing. For the assertion `assert "erk-plan" not in label_names`: change to `assert "erk-pr" not in label_names` if erk-pr is also identified by title prefix. But check the code first. IMPORTANT: Read each file carefully before making any edits. If something is unclear, make a conservative change that preserves intent.

def get_erk_label_definitions() -> list[LabelDefinition]: """Get all erk label definitions.
Returns list of LabelDefinition for all erk labels (erk-pr, erk-learn, erk-objective, no-changes). Used by init command to set up labels in target issue repositories. """ return [ LabelDefinition( name=_LABEL_ERK_PR, description=_LABEL_ERK_PR_DESC, color=_LABEL_ERK_PR_COLOR, ), LabelDefinition( name=_LABEL_ERK_LEARN, description=_LABEL_ERK_LEARN_DESC, color=_LABEL_ERK_LEARN_COLOR, ), LabelDefinition( name=_LABEL_ERK_OBJECTIVE, description=_LABEL_ERK_OBJECTIVE_DESC, color=_LABEL_ERK_OBJECTIVE_COLOR, ), LabelDefinition( name=_LABEL_NO_CHANGES, description=_LABEL_NO_CHANGES_DESC, color=_LABEL_NO_CHANGES_COLOR, ), ]
